data.title: Ethereum's Pectra Upgrade Launches on Holesky Testnet
data.description: According to Odaily, Ethereum's Pectra upgrade is set to go live on the Holesky testnet today at 9:55 PM UTC. This upgrade allows developers to test new improvements before they are implemented on the Ethereum mainnet. Pectra introduces features aimed at enhancing Ethereum accounts, supporting Layer 2 scaling, and improving the user experience for validators. The Ethereum Foundation highlights Pectra as a significant step towards account abstraction. A major change includes EIP-3074, which enhances Ethereum account abstraction by enabling externally owned accounts (EOA) to execute batch transactions and sponsor gas payments. Additionally, the Pectra upgrade will increase Ethereum's blob capacity by 50%. Currently, the mainnet supports an average of three blobs per block, which will rise to six following the Pectra deployment.

data.title: Canary Capital Advances HBAR ETF Proposal With SEC Filing
data.description:
According to ShibDaily, Canary Capital, a digital asset investment firm, has made significant progress in its efforts to launch a new exchange-traded fund (ETF) linked to Hedera's native utility token, HBAR. The firm has submitted an S-1 registration with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC), a crucial step in the ETF approval process. This move has generated considerable interest and speculation within the cryptocurrency community, as noted by Senior Bloomberg ETF analyst Eric Balchunas and Fox Business reporter Eleanor Terret.

The S-1 submission is an initial milestone in the process of launching the HBAR ETF. The next step for Canary Capital involves completing a 19b-4 filing, which will propose the necessary regulatory changes to list the fund on an exchange. The proposed HBAR ETF aims to provide investors with direct exposure to the altcoin, following the successful model established by Bitcoin ETFs such as Fidelity's FBTC and BlackRock's IBIT. Balchunas highlighted that Canary Capital submitted an amended S-1 for its HBAR ETF, a rare move typically made in response to SEC feedback. This mirrors a similar adjustment made with a Litecoin ETF, emphasizing the importance of the 19b-4 filing for advancing the process.

Canary Capital has previously received formal recognition from the SEC for a Litecoin ETF, making it the first altcoin fund, aside from Ethereum, to achieve such acknowledgment. This milestone sets a regulatory deadline for the SEC to either approve or reject the proposal, potentially transforming how investors access cryptocurrency markets. Despite the promising development of the revised S-1 filing, the essential Form 19b-4, required for ETF approval and submitted by the listing exchange, remains pending. This outstanding step indicates that the HBAR ETF still faces significant regulatory hurdles before it can be launched.

The investment firm's decision to replicate its Litecoin ETF process may suggest improved proficiency and streamlined operations in navigating regulatory complexities. However, the SEC continues to adopt a cautious stance toward cryptocurrency ETFs. Canary Capital's initiative coincides with a series of developments at the SEC, including an increase in new ETF applications, the establishment of a specialized unit to address crypto fraud and cybercrime, and the potential initiation of a probe by Elon Musk's Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE). This article is intended for informational purposes only and should not be considered financial advice. Readers are encouraged to conduct their own research and consult with a qualified financial adviser before making any investment decisions.

data.title: BNB Surpasses 640 USDT with a Narrowed 2.49% Decrease in 24 Hours
data.description: On Feb 24, 2025, 12:38 PM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, BNB has crossed the 640 USDT benchmark and is now trading at 640.289978 USDT, with a narrowed narrowed 2.49% decrease in 24 hours.

data.title: Ethereum Pectra Upgrade to Begin Testing on Holesky
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Ethereum's Pectra upgrade will go live on the Holesky testnet at 9:55 pm UTC today, allowing developers to test new improvements before they enter the Ethereum mainnet. Pectra introduces features that enhance Ethereum accounts, support L2 expansion, and improve the user experience of validators.
According to the Ethereum Foundation, Pectra represents an important step towards account abstraction. One of the major changes includes EIP-3074, which enhances Ethereum account abstraction by allowing externally owned accounts (EOA) to perform batch transactions and sponsor gas payments. In addition, the Pectra upgrade will increase Ethereum's blob capacity by 50%. The mainnet currently supports an average of 3 blobs per block, which will increase to 6 after Pectra is deployed. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: Bybit Quest to Reclaim $1.3B: Chasing Hack Funds and ETH Rollback
data.description:
Bybit CEO Ben Zhou has vowed to pursue every recovery option after a cyberattack linked to the Lazarus Group siphoned over $1.4 billion from the exchange. After a hacker seized an Ethereum cold wallet February 21, Zhou outlined the exchange’s response during an X Spaces session the next day. He explained that their plan involves launching a bounty program, partnering with law enforcement, and...
source: https://news.shib.io/2025/02/24/bybit-quest-to-reclaim-1-3b-chasing-hack-funds-and-eth-rollback/?utm_source=rss&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=bybit-quest-to-reclaim-1-3b-chasing-hack-funds-and-eth-rollback

data.title: South Dakota Bitcoin Reserve Bill Set For Committee Hearing
data.description: According to Odaily, a Bitcoin reserve bill in South Dakota is scheduled for a hearing today in the Commerce and Energy Committee. The bill's progress is being closely monitored as it could have significant implications for the state's approach to cryptocurrency regulation.

data.title: South Dakota’s Bitcoin Reserve Bill to Have Hearing in Commerce and Energy Committee
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily reported that according to Bitcoin Magazine, South Dakota's Bitcoin Reserve Act will hold a hearing in the Commerce and Energy Committee today.

data.title: LBank has launched SHADOW U-margined perpetual contract
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to the official announcement, LBank has launched the SHADOW U-based perpetual contract at 19:30 (SGT) on February 24, 2025, with a maximum leverage of 50 times.
